description_long: This is a traditional water well featuring a circular stone base constructed from dark, roughly hewn blocks, forming a sturdy and elevated perimeter. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof made of weathered planks, supported by two robust wooden posts on either side. A horizontal wooden crank handle is attached to one post, connected to a spindle mechanism for drawing water, with a visible winding rod extending down into the well. The interior of the well is open and unsealed, allowing access to the water below. The structure is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and the materials show signs of age and exposure to the elements. The overall design prioritizes function and durability, suitable for outdoor urban or park settings.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden pitched roof and a hand-crank mechanism for drawing water. The structure is robust, functional, and weathered, with no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden roof, and the crank handle on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone wall and the profile of the wooden roof.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front but with the crank handle less visible and the roof structure prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the circular winding mechanism and the side profile of the well.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden roof made of planks, supported by two posts; no lid or grating over the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Manual crank handle and winding rod mechanism present, made of wood and metal; no visible bucket.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, forming a protective barrier around the well opening; wooden roof provides overhead protection.
